target.name: Arbe Robotics
target.founder: Kobi Marenko, Noam Arkind, Oz Fixman

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $6.86B

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $3.33B

data.narrative:
Arbe Robotics is a public-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $118M to date. The comparable universe consists of seven private firms in the same sector, including Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, and Elroy Air. No public peers currently trade in this narrow subsegment, limiting the applicability of public market multiples.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The sector contains only five same-stage comparable companies, and filtering by adjacent stage or similar valuation tier yields fewer than the three datapoints required for statistical reliability. A broader sectoral median would obscure the distinct risk and growth profiles within autonomous vehicle subcategories and therefore introduces unacceptable noise into the analysis.

This analysis is constrained by the composition of the comparable set. The absence of public trading comps and the fragmented private market—where Joby, Archer, and others span eVTOL, last-mile delivery, and autonomous logistics with materially different unit economics—prevents triangulation to a defensible valuation range using standard methodologies. Further resolution requires either direct precedent transaction analysis, discounted cash flow modeling with sector-calibrated inputs, or expansion of the comp set to include earlier-stage funding rounds. Current data does not support a point estimate or narrow range recommendation.
